title: Design tokens: spacing/radius/shadow scale and typographic hierarchy
description: Fase 10 kickoff. The admin panel currently reads as a functional admin tool, not documentation software (per `plano-de-interface.md` and the Zeroheight-style references `referencia.png`/`referencia-2.png`). Before touching layout, establish the visual token foundation every later task in this phase builds on: a restrained spacing/radius/shadow scale, a near-white neutral palette with a single primary accent, and a typographic hierarchy (category/eyebrow, title, description, metadata, body) matching the plan's `# Tokens visuais` and `# Hierarquia tipográfica` sections. This is Tailwind theme + a couple of reusable text-style utilities/classes — no page is restructured yet (that starts at TASK-83).
acceptanceCriteria: Tailwind theme (in `apps/admin/src/index.css`'s `@theme` block or equivalent) defines the spacing scale (8/16/24/32/48/64px), radius scale (8/12px), and 1-2 very subtle shadow tokens from the plan, without touching the existing `.sb-admin`-scoped reset/preflight boundary established in TASK-75

Neutral color tokens lean toward white/near-white backgrounds (#FFFFFF / #FAFAFA), light borders (#EAEAEA-equivalent), and dark-gray text (#111111 primary / #666666 secondary) — reconciled with the existing shadcn `neutral` base color rather than introducing a second color system; a single primary accent color remains the only saturated color used for actions

Typographic hierarchy utilities/classes exist for: category label (12-14px, uppercase, tracked, muted), page title (40-48px, bold), description (22-26px, regular/light weight), metadata (14px, muted), and body content (16px, comfortable line-height) — documented once (e.g. a short comment block or a `typography.ts` constants file) so later tasks reuse them instead of re-deriving sizes

No visual regression in already-migrated screens (TASK-77..81) from this token pass alone — this task only adds/adjusts tokens, it does not yet restyle the header, sidebar, or content area (those are TASK-83..86)
The public documentation (`.sb-public`) and Tiptap prose (`editor.css`) remain completely unaffected, per the scoping already verified in TASK-75/81

steps.description: Audit current tokens vs. the plan
steps.details: Compare the current shadcn `neutral` theme (TASK-75) and any ad-hoc Tailwind classes in AdminLayout/SidebarTree/PageContentPage against `plano-de-interface.md`'s `# Tokens visuais` / `# Cores` / `# Hierarquia tipográfica` sections. List concrete gaps (missing radius/shadow tokens, no defined typographic scale).

Add spacing/radius/shadow custom properties to the `@theme` block, staying inside the `.sb-admin` scoping boundary from TASK-75. Keep the shadow tokens deliberately subtle (per plan: 'Sombras muito discretas. Nenhuma borda pesada.').

Create reusable Tailwind utility groupings or small helper components (e.g. `<Eyebrow>`, `<PageTitle>`, `<PageDescription>`, `<Metadata>`) for the 5 text roles in the plan. Prefer composable className constants over one-off inline classes so TASK-85's Section Header can consume them directly.
Load every admin screen and the public docs in the browser; confirm nothing shifted visually yet (this task is additive-only). Run `pnpm --filter @systembook/admin typecheck`.

technicalNotes: This is the foundation task for Fase 10 — keep it deliberately narrow (tokens + typography primitives only). Layout restructuring is explicitly out of scope here and belongs to TASK-83/84/85.
Reuse rather than replace: the shadcn `neutral` base color and CSS-var token mechanism from TASK-75 already exist — extend them, don't fork a parallel token system.

Reference images `referencia.png` (Zeroheight overview page) and `referencia-2.png` (block-insert menu) live at the repo root — use them for exact type-scale/color calibration, not just `plano-de-interface.md`'s prose ranges.
